definition:
User or service API key used specifically for LangSmith trace ingestion and dashboard access. Missing or invalid keys lead to external trace export failures even if local tracing is enabled.

Manage this key via secure r
LangTrace API endpoint host (optional). Stored in config field tracing.langtrace_api_host (and surfaced as LANGTRACE_API_HOST in env exports). Reserved for future external trace export.

Authentication token for LangTrace integration endpoints. Required when sending traces to LangTrace-hosted collectors or APIs.

Keep this token in secure env/secret storage and validate before enabling broad tracing in s
Project identifier for LangTrace (optional). Stored in config field tracing.langtrace_project_id (and surfaced as LANGTRACE_PROJECT_ID in env exports). Reserved for future external trace export.

How to ingest very large text files.

stream: reads in bounded character blocks (avoids loading the full file into memory).
read_all: reads the entire file into memory before chunking (simpler, but
When LARGE_FILE_MODE=stream, this controls the approximate number of characters to read per block. Larger blocks reduce boundary artifacts but increase memory usage.

Threshold for alerting on tail latency: if the 99th percentile (p99) of request latency over the monitoring window exceeds this value, an alert fires.

p99 focuses on the slowest 1% of requests. It’s useful for catching

Score boost applied to chunks from GUI/frontend layers when query intent is classified as UI-related. Part of the multi-layer architecture routing system. When users ask "how does the settings page work?" or "where is th

Score boost applied to chunks from backend/API/service layers when query intent is classified as retrieval or data-related. Complements LAYER_BONUS_GUI for multi-tier architecture routing. When users ask "how do we fetch

Backend used when RERANKER_MODE="learning". auto selects the MLX Qwen3 LoRA backend (Apple Silicon). mlx_qwen3 forces the same MLX backend. Legacy values (transformers/hf) are accepted for backward compatibility but norm

Base model identifier to fine-tune FROM when using the MLX Qwen3 learning backend (e.g. Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B). Training produces a LoRA adapter artifact written under TRIBRID_RERANKER_MODEL_PATH and inference loads t
Default pane preset applied when Learning Reranker Studio opens. Options: balanced, focus_viz, focus_logs, focus_inspector. Default: balanced.

Number of micro-batches to accumulate gradients over before applying one optimizer update when training the MLX Qwen3 learning reranker. This increases effective batch size without increasing memory. Typical: 4–16; defau
Layout system used by Learning Reranker Studio. Options: dockview or panels. Default: dockview. Use panels only if you need the simpler fallback layout behavior.
Preferred log output renderer in Learning Reranker Studio. Options: json or xterm. Default: xterm.
LoRA scaling (alpha) for MLX Qwen3 fine-tuning. Effective LoRA scale is alpha/rank. Typical: 16–64; start at 32.
LoRA dropout probability for MLX Qwen3 fine-tuning. Small dropout (0.0–0.1) can reduce overfitting on small mined datasets. Start at 0.05.
LoRA rank (r) for MLX Qwen3 fine-tuning. Higher rank increases adapter capacity (and training/inference cost) and can improve quality with enough data. Typical: 8–32; start at 16.

Comma-separated list of module names to apply LoRA to for the MLX Qwen3 learning backend (typical attention projections: q_proj,k_proj,v_proj,o_proj). Targeting more modules increases capacity but costs more memory/compu
